behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real Git rename semantics plus CLI parsing/output. This behavior depends on real repository state and Git’s rename detection output from subprocess/CLI interactions; mocks can hide format and threshold quirks. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is only about Git change categorization, with no AI/ML decision (so not Agentic) and no multi-agent sequence (so not Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI against actual git history, and assert report shows the file only in `renamed` with no corresponding `added`/`deleted` increment.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is typically encoding/subprocess/diff-format behavior with actual binary blobs. In-process fakes of diff text often miss real binary markers and subprocess output edge cases. Rejected higher levels: behavior text contains no model-dependent correctness and no multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: In a real git repo, add/modify a binary file (e.g., image), run the CLI, assert exit success, no crash, and binary file contributes to category counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: This boundary is real Git behavior for unborn `HEAD`/no-commit repos, often expressed via real command exit codes and stderr text. Mocking can hide exact subprocess failure modes. Rejected higher levels: behavior is Git/CLI error handling only, not AI/ML output and not agent chaining.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real git repo with zero commits, run the CLI, and assert it returns a valid empty structured report and non-error process outcome.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: Behavior validates end-to-end correctness against actual repository history, which depends on real git history traversal and category extraction under true repository state. Fakes risk matching synthetic fixtures while missing real history semantics. Rejected higher levels: no LLM/ML-dependent judgment and no multi-agent step sequence in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history containing known adds/deletes/renames/modifies across commits, run CLI, and assert each reported count matches the known ground truth from that history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real Git index vs working-tree semantics, which are OS/filesystem + git state interactions and commonly differ in real command behavior. In-process stubs can mask index/worktree discrepancies. Rejected higher levels: behavior text has no AI/ML dependency and no multi-step agent workflow.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create equivalent change sets once as staged and once as unstaged, run CLI for both states, and assert byte-for-byte identical structured stdout output.
